headline: Community Colleges Remain Underutilized at 58% Capacity Nationwide
context: Malaysia's 102 community colleges operate at 58% average enrollment capacity despite significant infrastructure investment.

cards.big: 102 community colleges operate at 58% capacity with 42,000 empty seats in 2025
cards.sub: RM3.2 billion invested in infrastructure since 2001 remains underutilized

cards.big: Only 34% of community college programs are aligned with local industry needs
cards.sub: Curriculum design is centralized in Putrajaya rather than responsive to regional demand

cards.big: Community college graduates earn average RM1,800 monthly versus RM2,800 for polytechnic graduates
cards.sub: Wage gap reduces incentive for students to choose community college pathways

cards.big: US community colleges serve 40% of all undergraduates through flexible local programming
cards.sub: Industry-responsive curriculum and credit transfer pathways drive enrollment

cards.big: Empty classrooms are not a marketing problem—they signal irrelevant course offerings
Should community colleges be governed locally to respond to regional employer needs
